cityCenter.name: Stare Miasto (Old Town)
cityCenter.description: Heart of Kraków at Rynek Główny, Europe's largest medieval market square

airports.name: Kraków–Balice
airports.fullName: John Paul II International Airport Kraków–Balice

routes.name: SKA1 Airport Train
routes.tips: A covered walkway connects the terminal to the airport rail platform. Buy from machines (cash/card) or tap contactless onboard; the train terminates at Kraków Główny in the city center.

routes.name: Bus 300 (express) / 209
routes.tips: Express line 300 is the quickest bus; line 209 makes more stops. The night line 902 covers the overnight gap, roughly every hour. Buy and validate a ticket onboard or at the stop.

routes.name: Taxi

price.note: Approx. 80-110 PLN to the Old Town depending on traffic

routes.tips: Use the official taxi rank outside arrivals or book a licensed company; agree the fare or ensure the meter is running. Ignore drivers soliciting inside the terminal.

routes.name: Bolt / Uber

price.note: Variable; usually cheaper than a metered taxi

routes.tips: Bolt and Uber are widely used and inexpensive in Kraków; the price is fixed in-app. Head to the designated rideshare pickup point outside the terminal.
cons: Pickup zone can be a short walk

generalTips: The SKA1 airport train to Kraków Główny is the fastest, most reliable way into the center (~20 min, 20 PLN); a covered walkway links the terminal to the platform.
Kraków Główny is the main rail hub, with frequent connections to Warsaw, Wrocław, Katowice, and Zakopane, plus the adjoining Galeria Krakowska mall and MDA bus station.
There is no metro—the city runs on MPK trams and buses. Download the Jakdojade app for routes, live times, and ticket purchase.
Always pay and be charged in PLN, never euros. Decline 'dynamic currency conversion' at ATMs and card terminals, and use bank ATMs rather than Euronet kiosks.
Consider the Kraków Tourist Card, which bundles unlimited public transport with free or discounted museum entry.
The Old Town inside the Planty ring is largely pedestrianized and walkable end to end in about 15 minutes—you won't need a car.
